hook.text: Yahoo tried to be everything: search, email, news, games, shopping, dating, and more. It ended up being nothing.

buildup.text: In 2000, Yahoo was the most visited website on the internet. Its homepage was a portal stuffed with every service imaginable.

discovery.text: Google arrived with a blank page. YouTube arrived with just video. Each focused competitor took a piece of Yahoo's territory.

twist.text: Yahoo had the chance to buy both Google and Facebook. It passed on both because it couldn't see past its portal strategy.

climax.text: Yahoo sold to Verizon in 2017 for $4.5 billion — a fraction of its $125 billion peak. Death by a thousand cuts.

punchline.text: Doing everything means doing nothing well enough to win.

quiz.question: What was Yahoo's fundamental product mistake?
options: Trying to be everything — focused competitors each captured a piece of its market
Charging too much for its services
